task_summary: Fix: Missing hello world startup log in oran-o2ims controller (openshift-kni/oran-o2ims)

messages.content:
Implement this task:

# Task: Fix: Missing hello world startup log in oran-o2ims controller (openshift-kni/oran-o2ims)

## Description
Bug: Missing hello world startup log in oran-o2ims controller   Repository: repo:openshift-kni/oran-o2ims   Fix Approach: Log separate 'Hello, World!' message before 'Starting manager'

The oran-o2ims controller is missing the required "Hello, World!" string in its startup logs. Currently, the controller logs "Starting manager" on startup (line 376-380 in internal/cmd/operator/start_controller_manager.go), but this does not include the mandatory greeting string. This violates a startup logging requirement.

Add a new logger.InfoContext(ctx, "Hello, World!") call immediately before the existing "Starting manager" log statement. This creates two distinct log entries: 1. Greeting log: "Hello, World!" 2. Status log: "Starting manager" with image metadata

Tradeoffs: - ✅ Separates concerns (greeting vs operational status) - ✅ Ensures greeting is always visible - ✅ Clear indication both requirements are met - ⚠️ Adds one additional log entry (minimal overhead)

File: internal/cmd/operator/start_controller_manager.go

Action: - Navigate to the run() function - Identify lines 376-380 where logger.InfoContext() logs "Starting manager" - Review the surrounding context to understand:   - Variable names (confirm ctx and logger are in scope)   - Goroutine structure   - Any error handling that might affect log execution

Verification:

Location: Immediately before line 376 (the "Starting manager" log)

Code Change:

Implementation: - Insert a new line immediately before the existing logger.InfoContext() call - Use the same logger instance and ctx context - Keep the message simple: exactly "Hello, World!" - No additional fields needed for the greeting

Indentation: Match the existing code style (likely tabs, consistent with Go conventions)

Code Review Checklist: - [ ] "Hello, World!" log is called before "Starting manager" - [ ] Both logs use the same context (ctx) - [ ] No changes to existing "Starting manager" log - [ ] Indentation matches surrounding code - [ ] No syntax errors introduced

Build Validation:

Expected Outcome: - Build succeeds without errors - No linting warnings for the modified lines

Format the file:

Lint (if configured):

Manual Verification: Since this is a startup log change, verification requires running the controller:

Expected Log Output:

Note: Actual testing may be handled in a separate test task. For the implementation, ensure: - Code compiles - Syntax is correct - Change is minimal and isolated

Files to commit: - internal/cmd/operator/start_controller_manager.go

Commit message:

Commit process:

Risk Level: LOW

Justification: - Minimal code change (one line addition) - No modifications to existing logic - Log statements have no side effects - No changes to control flow or error handling

Potential Issues: 1. Nil logger: If logger is nil, will panic    - Mitigation: Existing "Starting manager" log would already fail; this is not a new risk 2. Context issues: If ctx is nil or canceled    - Mitigation: Same as above; existing code already depends on valid context 3. Log volume: One additional log line per startup    - Mitigation: Negligible impact; startup logs are infrequent

Required: - Access to repo:openshift-kni/oran-o2ims - Go toolchain (for build validation) - Git (for committing)

Optional: - Development environment for runtime testing - CI/CD pipeline access for automated testing

If the change causes issues:

The oran-o2ims controller logs 'Starting manager' on startup but is missing the required 'Hello, World!' string. This log message was introduced in commit 7e1c10261 on April 17, 2024, without the 'Hello, World!' string, and has remained in this state through subsequent modifications including being moved into a goroutine in commit c6d715490. The missing string violates the requirement that it must always be present on startup.

internal/cmd/operator/start_controller_manager.go:run (lines 376-380)

The controller manager's run function logs a startup message using logger.InfoContext() with the message 'Starting manager' and the image parameter, but does not include 'Hello, World!' in the log message or as an additional field. The InfoContext call at line 376-380 only passes the context, message string, and image field, with no 'Hello, World!' string present.

When the oran-o2ims controller starts up, the run() method is called, which initializes the controller manager and starts it in a goroutine. Before starting the manager, it calls logger.InfoContext() to log the startup message. This produces a log entry containing 'Starting manager' and the image information, but lacking the required 'Hello, World!' string. Operators reviewing startup logs see the 'Starting manager' message but not the crucial 'Hello, World!' greeting.

commit 7e1c10261942762ff52c6ba5a1bbfd77839c89e7 (2024-04-17), PR #91

High (100%) — The code location is directly confirmed by examining the source file at lines 376-380. Git blame conclusively traces the log message to commits c6d715490 (structure change) and the underlying message to 7e1c10261 (initial creation). The absence of 'Hello, World!' is verified by direct code inspection and comprehensive repository search.

Log separate 'Hello, World!' message before 'Starting manager': Add a new logger.InfoContext(ctx, 'Hello, World!') call immediately before the existing 'Starting manager' log at line 376. This creates two distinct log entries: one with the greeting and one with the manager startup information.

Scope: This task covers changes to openshift-kni/oran-o2ims only.

## Project Guidelines
# Agent Guidelines

# AGENTS.md

This file provides codebase guidance for AI coding agents working with this repository.

## Project Overview

O-Cloud Manager operator built on OpenShift and ACM (Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management). Implements the O-RAN O2 IMS specification for 5G infrastructure management: bare-metal inventory, cluster provisioning, firmware management, and alarm monitoring via REST APIs.

## Development Commands

Use `make help` to list all available targets. Key commands:

- `make ci-job` - Full CI pipeline locally (format, vet, lint, test, e2e, envtest, coverage, bundle-check). Run before submitting PRs. Includes `bundle-check`, which verifies the git tree is clean after code generation — a common CI failure when API or manifest changes aren't regenerated.
- `make generate && make manifests && make bundle` - Regenerate code after API changes.

### Lint by File Type

Run targeted checks instead of waiting for the full `ci-job`:

- Go files: `make golangci-lint`
- YAML files: `make yamllint`
- Shell scripts: `make shellcheck bashate`
- Markdown files: `make markdownlint` (not included in `ci-job` — run separately)
- All of the above except markdownlint: `make lint`

### Running a Single Test

Pass Ginkgo flags via `ginkgo_flags`:

```bash
make test ginkgo_flags="--focus='should handle alarm'"
make test-envtest ginkgo_flags="--focus='InventoryController'"
```

### Container Build

To build and push to a personal registry, set `IMAGE_TAG_BASE` and `VERSION`:

```bash
make IMAGE_TAG_BASE=quay.io/<your-username>/oran-o2ims VERSION=latest docker-build docker-push
```

## Architecture

### Multi-Service Binary

The project builds a single binary that runs as multiple services in separate containers. Each service has its own cmd package at `internal/service/{service}/cmd/`. The Deployment determines which subcommand each container runs.

### Service Architecture Pattern

Services follow a consistent initialization pattern in `internal/service/{service}/`:

1. Embedded OpenAPI spec for request validation
2. PostgreSQL connection pool (services with persistence)
3. Repository layer implementing a defined interface (`db/repo/`)
4. Infrastructure clients for cross-service communication
5. Authentication/authorization middleware
6. HTTP server with generated OpenAPI handlers

Shared infrastructure lives in `internal/service/common/` (middleware, DB helpers, server config).

REST API code under `generated/` is auto-generated from OpenAPI specs via `//go:generate`. Don't edit generated files — edit the `openapi.yaml` and run `make go-generate`.

### Provisioning Workflow Phases

The ProvisioningRequestController runs a multi-phase state machine:

1. **Validation** - Validate request against ClusterTemplate schema
2. **Hardware Provisioning** - Create NodeAllocationRequest, watch NAR status, create BMC secrets
3. **Cluster Installation** - Render and apply ClusterInstance, monitor ZTP progress
4. **Post-Provisioning** - Apply policy templates, monitor compliance
5. **Upgrades** - Handle cluster upgrade requests

Each phase sets typed conditions on the CR status. Condition helpers are in `internal/controllers/utils/conditions.go`. Condition types and reasons are defined in `api/provisioning/v1alpha1/conditions.go`.

### Database Schema Changes

This project has not reached GA, so there are no production databases to migrate. Do not add new incremental migration files. Instead, modify the existing baseline files in `internal/service/{service}/db/migrations/` in place.

### API Groups and Key CRDs

- `clcm.openshift.io` - ClusterTemplate, ProvisioningRequest, HardwareProfile, NodeAllocationRequest, AllocatedNode
- `ocloud.openshift.io` - Inventory

### Design Decisions

The following are accepted design decisions. Do not flag these patterns as
issues during code review.

- **DD-002: Cluster-scoped ProvisioningRequest watch mappers.**
  Watch mappers that enqueue ProvisioningRequests (e.g.,
  `enqueueProvisioningRequestForNAR`) intentionally omit the namespace from
  `NamespacedName`. `ProvisioningRequest` is cluster-scoped (`scope: Cluster`
  in the CRD) and has no namespace.

## Test Conventions

- Use `.ToNot()` for negated Gomega assertions, not `.NotTo()` or
  `.ShouldNot()`. The codebase is standardized on `.ToNot()`.
- Use `Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())` for asserting no error, not
  `Expect(err).To(BeNil())`. `HaveOccurred()` produces better failure
  messages (prints the error vs just "expected nil").

## Contributing Requirements

- All commits must be signed off with DCO: `git commit -s`
- Commit all files and run `make ci-job` before submitting PRs
- After API changes: `make generate && make manifests && make bundle`
- AI-generated code must use `Co-Authored-By` or `Assisted-By` trailer
- Run lint checks before committing (see [Lint by File Type](#lint-by-file-type))
- When making code changes, ensure test coverage for new code and functional
  changes. If a bug fix or new behavior is added without a corresponding test,
  write one. If an existing scenario is discovered to be untested (e.g., during
  code review), add a test for it in the same commit or PR. Tests should verify
  the specific behavior, not just increase line coverage.


## Instructions
1. Read and understand the existing codebase
2. Implement the task following the repository's coding standards
3. Write clean, well-documented code
4. Run tests to verify your changes work
5. Commit your changes with a descriptive message

1	/*

     6	
     7	package operator
     8	
     9	import (
    10		"context"
    11		"crypto/tls"
    12		"flag"
    13		"log/slog"
    14	
    15		openshiftv1 "github.com/openshift/api/config/v1"
    16		openshiftoperatorv1 "github.com/openshift/api/operator/v1"
    17		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics/filters"
    18	
    19		"github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/internal/constants"
    20		ctlrutils "github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/internal/controllers/utils"
    21	
    22		// Import all Kubernetes client auth plugins (e.g. Azure, GCP, OIDC, etc.)
    23		// to ensure that exec-entrypoint and run can make use of them.
    24		_ "k8s.io/client-go/plugin/pkg/client/auth"
    25		"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    26	
    27		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
    28		utilruntime "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime"
    29		clientgoscheme "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme"
    30		ctrl "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime"
    31		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/healthz"
    32		metricsserver "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics/server"
    33		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook"
    34	
    35		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
    36	
    37		metal3v1alpha1 "github.com/metal3-io/baremetal-operator/apis/metal3.io/v1alpha1"
    38		ibguv1alpha1 "github.com/openshift-kni/cluster-group-upgrades-operator/pkg/api/imagebasedgroupupgrades/v1alpha1"
    39		hwmgmtv1alpha1 "github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/api/hardwaremanagement/v1alpha1"
    40		inventoryv1alpha1 "github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/api/inventory/v1alpha1"
    41		provisioningv1alpha1 "github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/api/provisioning/v1alpha1"
    42		svcutils "github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/internal/service/common/utils"
    43		assistedservicev1beta1 "github.com/openshift/assisted-service/api/v1beta1"
    44		hivev1 "github.com/openshift/hive/apis/hive/v1"
    45		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    46		observabilityv1beta1 "github.com/stolostron/multicluster-observability-operator/operators/multiclusterobservability/api/v1beta1"
    47		siteconfig "github.com/stolostron/siteconfig/api/v1alpha1"
    48		apiextensionsv1 "k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/v1"
    49		clusterv1 "open-cluster-management.io/api/cluster/v1"
    50		policiesv1 "open-cluster-management.io/governance-policy-propagator/api/v1"
    51	
    52		"github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/internal"
    53		"github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/internal/controllers"
    54		"github.com/openshift-kni/oran-o2ims/internal/exit"
    55	)
    56	
    57	// ControllerManager creates and returns the `start controller-manager` command.
    58	func ControllerManager() *cobra.Command {
    59		c := NewControllerManager()
    60		result := &cobra.Command{
    61			Use:   "controller-manager",
    62			Short: "Starts the controller manager",
    63			Args:  cobra.NoArgs,
    64			RunE:  c.run,
    65		}
    66		flags := result.Flags()
    67		flags.StringVar(
    68			&c.metricsAddr,
    69			"metrics-bind-address",
    70			constants.MetricsPort,
    71			"The address the metric endpoint binds to.",
    72		)
    73		flags.StringVar(
    74			&c.metricsCertDir,
    75			"metrics-tls-cert-dir",
    76			"",
    77			"The directory containing the tls.crt and tls.key.",
    78		)
    79		flags.StringVar(
    80			&c.probeAddr,
    81			"health-probe-bind-address",
    82			constants.HealthProbePort,
    83			"The address the probe endpoint binds to.",
    84		)
    85		flag.BoolVar(
    86			&c.enableHTTP2,
    87			"enable-http2", false,
    88			"If set, HTTP/2 will be enabled for the metrics and webhook servers",
    89		)
    90		flags.BoolVar(
    91			&c.enableLeaderElection,
    92			"leader-elect",
    93			false,
    94			"Enable leader election for controller manager. "+
    95				"Enabling this will ensure there is only one active controller manager.",
    96		)
    97		flags.BoolVar(&c.enableWebhooks,
    98			"enable-webhooks",
    99			true,
   100			"Enable the o2ims validating webhooks")

101		flags.StringVar(
   102			&c.image,
   103			imageFlagName,
   104			// Intentionally setting the default value to "" if the environment variable is not set to ensure we never
   105			// run an image that we didn't intend on running.
   106			ctlrutils.GetEnvOrDefault(constants.ServerImageName, ""),
   107			"Reference of the container image containing the servers.",
   108		)
   109		return result
   110	}
   111	
   112	// ControllerManagerCommand contains the data and logic needed to run the `start controller-manager`
   113	// command.
   114	type ControllerManagerCommand struct {
   115		metricsAddr          string
   116		metricsCertDir       string
   117		enableHTTP2          bool
   118		enableLeaderElection bool
   119		enableWebhooks       bool
   120		probeAddr            string
   121		image                string
   122		svcutils.CommonServerConfig
   123	}
   124	
   125	// NewControllerManager creates a new runner that knows how to execute the `start
   126	// controller-manager` command.
   127	func NewControllerManager() *ControllerManagerCommand {
   128		return &ControllerManagerCommand{}
   129	}
   130	
   131	var (
   132		scheme = runtime.NewScheme()
   133	)
   134	
   135	func init() {
   136		utilruntime.Must(clientgoscheme.AddToScheme(scheme))
   137		utilruntime.Must(provisioningv1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   138		utilruntime.Must(inventoryv1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   139		utilruntime.Must(siteconfig.AddToScheme(scheme))
   140		utilruntime.Must(hwmgmtv1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   141		utilruntime.Must(hwmgmtv1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   142		utilruntime.Must(clusterv1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   143		utilruntime.Must(policiesv1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   144		utilruntime.Must(openshiftv1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   145		utilruntime.Must(openshiftoperatorv1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   146		utilruntime.Must(ibguv1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   147		utilruntime.Must(assistedservicev1beta1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   148		utilruntime.Must(apiextensionsv1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   149		utilruntime.Must(metal3v1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   150		utilruntime.Must(observabilityv1beta1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   151		utilruntime.Must(hivev1.AddToScheme(scheme))
   152	}
   153	
   154	// run executes the `start controller-manager` command.
   155	func (c *ControllerManagerCommand) run(cmd *cobra.Command, argv []string) error {
   156		// Get the context:
   157		ctx := cmd.Context()
   158	
   159		// Get the dependencies from the context:
   160		logger := internal.LoggerFromContext(ctx)
   161	
   162		// Configure klog to use our structured logger for vendor modules:
   163		klog.SetSlogLogger(logger)
   164	
   165		// Configure the controller runtime library to use our logger:
   166		adapter := logr.FromSlogHandler(logger.Handler())
   167		ctrl.SetLogger(adapter)
   168		klog.SetLogger(adapter)
   169	
   170		// Check the flags:
   171		if c.image == "" {
   172			logger.ErrorContext(
   173				ctx,
   174				"Image flag is mandatory",
   175				slog.String("flag", imageFlagName),
   176			)
   177			return exit.Error(1)
   178		}
   179	
   180		// Set up common server configuration early so it's available for all servers
   181		if err := svcutils.SetCommonServerFlags(cmd, &c.CommonServerConfig); err != nil {
   182			return err //nolint:wrapcheck
   183		}
   184	
   185		// Set the TLS options.
   186		// If the enable-http2 flag is false (the default), http/2 will be disabled due to its vulnerabilities.
   187		// More specifically, disabling http/2 will prevent from being vulnerable to the HTTP/2 Stream
   188		// Cancelation and Rapid Reset CVEs. For more information see:
   189		// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qppj-fm5r-hxr3
   190		// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4374-p667-p6c8
   191		tlsOpts := []func(*tls.Config){}
   192		if !c.enableHTTP2 {
   193			tlsOpts = append(tlsOpts, func(c *tls.Config) {
   194				logger.InfoContext(ctx, "disabling http/2")
   195				c.NextProtos = []string{"http/1.1"}
   196			})
   197		}
   198	
   199		mgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{
   200			Scheme: scheme,
   201			Metrics: metricsserver.Options{
   202				SecureServing:  c.metricsCertDir != "",
   203				CertDir:        c.metricsCertDir,
   204				BindAddress:    c.metricsAddr,
   205				TLSOpts:        tlsOpts,
   206				FilterProvider: filters.WithAuthenticationAndAuthorization,
   207			},
   208			HealthProbeBindAddress: c.probeAddr,
   209			LeaderElection:         c.enableLeaderElection,
   210			LeaderElectionID:       "a73bc4d2.openshift.io",
   211			WebhookServer: webhook.NewServer(
   212				webhook.Options{
   213					TLSOpts: tlsOpts,
   214				},
   215			),
   216	
   217			// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel defines if the leader should step down voluntarily
   218			// when the Manager ends. This requires the binary to immediately end when the
   219			// Manager is stopped, otherwise, this setting is unsafe. Setting this significantly
   220			// speeds up voluntary leader transitions as the new leader don't have to wait
   221			// LeaseDuration time first.
   222			//
   223			// In the default scaffold provided, the program ends immediately after
   224			// the manager stops, so would be fine to enable this option. However,
   225			// if you are doing or is intended to do any operation such as perform cleanups
   226			// after the manager stops then its usage might be unsafe.
   227			// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel: true,
   228		})
   229		if err != nil {
   230			logger.ErrorContext(
   231				ctx,
   232				"Unable to start manager",
   233				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   234			)
   235			return exit.Error(1)
   236		}
   237	
   238		// Create the default inventory CR
   239		err = ctlrutils.CreateDefaultInventoryCR(ctx, mgr.GetClient())
   240		if err != nil {
   241			logger.ErrorContext(
   242				ctx,
   243				"Failed to create default inventory CR",
   244				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   245			)
   246			return exit.Error(1)
   247		}
   248	
   249		// Start the O-Cloud Manager controller.
   250		if err = (&controllers.Reconciler{

251			Client: mgr.GetClient(),
   252			Logger: logger.With("controller", "O-Cloud Manager"),
   253			Image:  c.image,
   254		}).SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {
   255			logger.ErrorContext(
   256				ctx,
   257				"Unable to create controller",
   258				slog.String("controller", "O-Cloud Manager"),
   259				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   260			)
   261			return exit.Error(1)
   262		}
   263	
   264		// Register field indexes for hierarchy CRs (Location, OCloudSite, ResourcePool) for
   265		// efficient lookups
   266		if err := ctlrutils.SetupHierarchyIndexers(ctx, mgr); err != nil {
   267			logger.ErrorContext(ctx, "Unable to set up hierarchy indexers", slog.Any("error", err))
   268			return exit.Error(1)
   269		}
   270	
   271		// Start the Cluster Template controller.
   272		if err = (&controllers.ClusterTemplateReconciler{
   273			Client: mgr.GetClient(),
   274			Logger: logger.With("controller", "ClusterTemplate"),
   275		}).SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {
   276			logger.ErrorContext(
   277				ctx,
   278				"Unable to create controller",
   279				slog.String("controller", "ClusterTemplate"),
   280				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   281			)
   282			return exit.Error(1)
   283		}
   284	
   285		// Start the Location controller.
   286		if err = (&controllers.LocationReconciler{
   287			Client: mgr.GetClient(),
   288			Logger: logger.With("controller", "Location"),
   289		}).SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {
   290			logger.ErrorContext(
   291				ctx,
   292				"Unable to create controller",
   293				slog.String("controller", "Location"),
   294				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   295			)
   296			return exit.Error(1)
   297		}
   298	
   299		// Start the OCloudSite controller.
   300		if err = (&controllers.OCloudSiteReconciler{
   301			Client: mgr.GetClient(),
   302			Logger: logger.With("controller", "OCloudSite"),
   303		}).SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {
   304			logger.ErrorContext(
   305				ctx,
   306				"Unable to create controller",
   307				slog.String("controller", "OCloudSite"),
   308				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   309			)
   310			return exit.Error(1)
   311		}
   312	
   313		// Start the ResourcePool controller.
   314		if err = (&controllers.ResourcePoolReconciler{
   315			Client: mgr.GetClient(),
   316			Logger: logger.With("controller", "ResourcePool"),
   317		}).SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {
   318			logger.ErrorContext(
   319				ctx,
   320				"Unable to create controller",
   321				slog.String("controller", "ResourcePool"),
   322				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   323			)
   324			return exit.Error(1)
   325		}
   326	
   327		serverErrors := make(chan error, 1)
   328	
   329		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
   330		defer cancel()
   331	
   332		// Start the Provisioning Request controller.
   333		if err = (&controllers.ProvisioningRequestReconciler{
   334			Client: mgr.GetClient(),
   335			Logger: logger.With("controller", "ProvisioningRequest"),
   336		}).SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {
   337			logger.ErrorContext(
   338				ctx,
   339				"Unable to create controller",
   340				slog.String("controller", "ProvisioningRequest"),
   341				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   342			)
   343			return exit.Error(1)
   344		}
   345	
   346		if c.enableWebhooks {
   347			if err = (&provisioningv1alpha1.ProvisioningRequest{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr); err != nil {
   348				logger.ErrorContext(
   349					ctx,
   350					"Unable to create webhook",
   351					slog.String("webhook", "ProvisioningRequest"),
   352					slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   353				)
   354				return exit.Error(1)
   355			}
   356		}
   357	
   358		if err := mgr.AddHealthzCheck("healthz", healthz.Ping); err != nil {
   359			logger.ErrorContext(
   360				ctx,
   361				"Unable to set up health check",
   362				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   363			)
   364			return exit.Error(1)
   365		}
   366		if err := mgr.AddReadyzCheck("readyz", healthz.Ping); err != nil {
   367			logger.ErrorContext(
   368				ctx,
   369				"Unable to set up ready check",
   370				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   371			)
   372			return exit.Error(1)
   373		}
   374	
   375		go func() {
   376			logger.InfoContext(ctx, "Hello, World!")
   377			logger.InfoContext(
   378				ctx,
   379				"Starting manager",
   380				slog.String("image", c.image),
   381			)
   382			if err := mgr.Start(ctrl.SetupSignalHandler()); err != nil {
   383				logger.ErrorContext(ctx, "Problem running manager", slog.String("error", err.Error()))
   384				serverErrors <- err
   385				return
   386			}
   387			// The manager has terminated normally. Cancel the context to allow the API server to shutdown
   388			cancel()
   389		}()
   390	
   391		select {
   392		case err = <-serverErrors:
   393			// Server failed to start
   394			logger.ErrorContext(ctx, "Problem running internal server", slog.String("error", err.Error()))
   395			return exit.Error(1)
   396		case <-ctx.Done():
   397			return exit.Error(0)
   398		}
   399	}
   400
